info.title: Amazon Lambda Tools for .NET Core applications (6.0.5)
info.description:
Project Home: https://github.com/aws/aws-extensions-for-dotnet-cli, https://github.com/aws/aws-lambda-dotnet
Commands to deploy and manage AWS Lambda functions:
deploy-function         Command to deploy the project to AWS Lambda
invoke-function         Command to invoke a function in Lambda with an optional input
list-functions          Command to list all your Lambda functions
delete-function         Command to delete a Lambda function
get-function-config     Command to get the current runtime configuration for a Lambda function
update-function-config  Command to update the runtime configuration for a Lambda function
Commands to deploy and manage AWS Serverless applications using AWS CloudFormation:
deploy-serverless       Command to deploy an AWS Serverless application
list-serverless         Command to list all your AWS Serverless applications
delete-serverless       Command to delete an AWS Serverless application
Commands to publish and manage AWS Lambda Layers:
publish-layer           Command to publish a Layer that can be associated with a Lambda function
list-layers             Command to list Layers
list-layer-versions     Command to list versions for a Layer
get-layer-version       Command to get the details of a Layer version
delete-layer-version    Command to delete a version of a Layer
Other Commands:
package                 Command to package a Lambda project either into a zip file or docker image if --package-type is set to "image". The output can later be deployed to Lambda with either deploy-function command or with another tool.
package-ci              Command to use as part of a continuous integration system.
push-image              Build Lambda Docker image and push the image to Amazon ECR.
To get help on individual commands execute:
dotnet lambda help <command>

commands.name: deploy-function
commands.description: Command to deploy the project to AWS Lambda

options.name: --disable-interactive
options.description: When set to true missing required parameters will not be prompted for.

options.name: --region
options.description: The region to connect to AWS services, if not set region will be detected from the environment.

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to use to look up AWS credentials, if not set environment credentials will be used.

options.name: --profile-location
options.description: Optional override to the search location for Profiles, points at a shared credentials file.

options.name: --aws-access-key-id
options.description: The AWS access key id. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-secret-key
options.description: The AWS secret key. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-session-token
options.description: The AWS session token. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: -pl
options.description: | --project-location             The location of the project, if not set the current directory will be assumed.

options.name: -cfg
options.description: | --config-file                  Configuration file storing default values for command line arguments.

options.name: -pcfg
options.description: | --persist-config-file          If true the arguments used for a successful deployment are persisted to a config file.

options.name: -c
options.description: | --configuration                Configuration to build with, for example Release or Debug.

options.name: -f
options.description: | --framework                    Target framework to compile, for example netcoreapp3.1.

options.name: --msbuild-parameters
options.description: Additional msbuild parameters passed to the 'dotnet publish' command. Add quotes around the value if the value contains spaces.

options.name: -pac
options.description: | --package                      Application package to use for deployment, skips building the project

options.name: -fn
options.description: | --function-name                AWS Lambda function name

options.name: -fd
options.description: | --function-description         AWS Lambda function description

options.name: -pt
options.description: | --package-type                 The deployment package type for Lambda function. Valid values: image, zip

options.name: -fp
options.description: | --function-publish             Publish a new version as an atomic operation

options.name: -fms
options.description: | --function-memory-size         The amount of memory, in MB, your Lambda function is given

options.name: --function-role
options.description: The IAM role that Lambda assumes when it executes your function

options.name: -ft
options.description: | --function-timeout             The function execution timeout in seconds

options.name: -fh
options.description: | --function-handler             Handler for the function. .NET Class libraries use the format <assembly>::<type>::<method> and executables use the format <assembly>

options.name: -frun
options.description: | --function-runtime             The runtime environment for the Lambda function

options.name: --function-architecture
options.description: The architecture of the Lambda function. Valid values: x86_64 or arm64. Default is x86_64

options.name: -fl
options.description: | --function-layers              Comma delimited list of Lambda layer version arns

options.name: -ie
options.description: | --image-entrypoint             Overrides the image's ENTRYPOINT when package type is set "image".

options.name: -ic
options.description: | --image-command                Overrides the image's CMD when package type is set "image".

options.name: -iwd
options.description: | --image-working-directory      Overrides the image's working directory when package type is set "image".

options.name: -it
options.description: | --image-tag                    Name and optionally a tag in the 'name:tag' format.

options.name: --ephemerals-storage-size
options.description: The size of the function's /tmp directory in MB. The default value is 512, but can be any whole number between 512 and 10240 MB

options.name: --function-url-enable
options.description: Enable function URL. A function URL is a dedicated HTTP(S) endpoint for your Lambda function.

options.name: --function-url-auth
options.description: The type of authentication that your function URL uses, default value is NONE. Valid values: NONE or AWS_IAM

options.name: --tags
options.description: AWS tags to apply. Format is <name1>=<value1>;<name2>=<value2>

options.name: -fsub
options.description: | --function-subnets             Comma delimited list of subnet ids if your function references resources in a VPC

options.name: -fsec
options.description: | --function-security-groups     Comma delimited list of security group ids if your function references resources in a VPC

options.name: -dlta
options.description: | --dead-letter-target-arn       Target ARN of an SNS topic or SQS Queue for the Dead Letter Queue

options.name: -tm
options.description: | --tracing-mode                 Configures when AWS X-Ray should trace the function. Valid values: PassThrough or Active

options.name: -ev
options.description: | --environment-variables        Environment variables set for the function. For existing functions this replaces the current environment variables. Format is <key1>=<value1>;<key2>=<value2>

options.name: -aev
options.description: | --append-environment-variables Append environment variables to the existing set of environment variables for the function. Format is <key1>=<value1>;<key2>=<value2>

options.name: -kk
options.description: | --kms-key                      KMS Key ARN of a customer key used to encrypt the function's environment variables

options.name: --apply-defaults
options.description: Obsolete: as of version 3.0.0.0 defaults are always applied.

options.name: -rs
options.description: | --resolve-s3                   If set to true a bucket with the name format of "aws-dotnet-lambda-tools-<region>-<account-id>" will be configured to store build outputs

options.name: -sb
options.description: | --s3-bucket                    S3 bucket to upload the build output

options.name: -sp
options.description: | --s3-prefix                    S3 prefix for for the build output

options.name: -dvc
options.description: | --disable-version-check        Disable the .NET Core version check. Only for advanced usage.

options.name: -ldi
options.description: | --local-docker-image           If set the docker build command is skipped and the indicated local image is pushed to ECR.

options.name: -df
options.description: | --dockerfile                   The docker file used to build the image. Default value is "Dockerfile".

options.name: -dbo
options.description: | --docker-build-options         Additional options passed to the "docker build" command.

options.name: -dbwd
options.description: | --docker-build-working-dir     The directory to execute the "docker build" command from.

options.name: --docker-host-build-output-dir
options.description: If set a "dotnet publish" command is executed on the host machine before executing "docker build". The output can be copied into image being built.

options.name: -ucfb
options.description: | --use-container-for-build      Use a local container to build the Lambda binary. A default image will be provided if none is supplied.

options.name: -cifb
options.description: | --container-image-for-build    The container image tag (with version) to be used for building the Lambda binary.

options.name: -cmd
options.description: | --code-mount-directory         Path to the directory to mount to the build container. Otherwise, look upward for a solution folder.

options.name: -lf
options.description: | --log-format                   The log format used by the Lambda function. Valid values are: Text or JSON. Default is Text

options.name: -lal
options.description: | --log-application-level        The log level. Valid values are: TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR or FATAL. Default is INFO.

options.name: -lsl
options.description: | --log-system-level             The log system level. Valid values are: DEBUG, INFO, WARN. Default is INFO.

options.name: -lg
options.description: | --log-group                    The name of the Amazon CloudWatch log group the function sends logs to. Default is /aws/lambda/<function name>.

options.name: -sa
options.description: | --snap-start-apply-on          Configure when a snapshot of the initialized execution environment should be taken. Valid values are: PublishedVersions, None. Default is None.

options.description: | --payload                      The input payload to send to the Lambda function

commands.name: deploy-serverless
commands.description: Command to deploy an AWS Serverless application

options.name: --disable-interactive
options.description: When set to true missing required parameters will not be prompted for.

options.name: --region
options.description: The region to connect to AWS services, if not set region will be detected from the environment.

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to use to look up AWS credentials, if not set environment credentials will be used.

options.name: --profile-location
options.description: Optional override to the search location for Profiles, points at a shared credentials file.

options.name: --aws-access-key-id
options.description: The AWS access key id. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-secret-key
options.description: The AWS secret key. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-session-token
options.description: The AWS session token. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: -pl
options.description: | --project-location             The location of the project, if not set the current directory will be assumed.

options.name: -cfg
options.description: | --config-file                  Configuration file storing default values for command line arguments.

options.name: -pcfg
options.description: | --persist-config-file          If true the arguments used for a successful deployment are persisted to a config file.

options.name: -c
options.description: | --configuration                Configuration to build with, for example Release or Debug.

options.name: -f
options.description: | --framework                    Target framework to compile, for example netcoreapp3.1.

options.name: --msbuild-parameters
options.description: Additional msbuild parameters passed to the 'dotnet publish' command. Add quotes around the value if the value contains spaces.

options.name: -pac
options.description: | --package                      Application package to use for deployment, skips building the project

options.name: -rs
options.description: | --resolve-s3                   If set to true a bucket with the name format of "aws-dotnet-lambda-tools-<region>-<account-id>" will be configured to store build outputs

options.name: -sb
options.description: | --s3-bucket                    S3 bucket to upload the build output

options.name: -sp
options.description: | --s3-prefix                    S3 prefix for for the build output

options.name: -t
options.description: | --template                     Path to the CloudFormation template

options.name: -tp
options.description: | --template-parameters          CloudFormation template parameters. Format is <key1>=<value1>;<key2>=<value2>

options.name: -ts
options.description: | --template-substitutions       JSON based CloudFormation template substitutions. Format is <JSONPath>=<Substitution>;<JSONPath>=...

options.name: --cloudformation-role
options.description: Optional role that CloudFormation assumes when creating or updated CloudFormation stack.

options.name: -sn
options.description: | --stack-name                   CloudFormation stack name for an AWS Serverless application

options.name: -dc
options.description: | --disable-capabilities         Comma delimited list of capabilities to disable when creating a CloudFormation Stack.

options.name: --tags
options.description: AWS tags to apply. Format is <name1>=<value1>;<name2>=<value2>

options.name: -sw
options.description: | --stack-wait                   If true wait for the Stack to finish updating before exiting. Default is true.

options.name: -dvc
options.description: | --disable-version-check        Disable the .NET Core version check. Only for advanced usage.

options.name: -pd
options.description: | --stack-polling-delay          The time interval in seconds between each check for stack updates. Default is 3 seconds.

arguments.name: STACK-NAME> THE NAME OF THE CLOUDFORMATION STACK USED TO DEPLOY THE AWS SERVERLESS APPLICATION

options.description: AWS Lambda layer name

options.name: --layer-type
options.description: The type of layer to publish. Valid values are: runtime-package-store

options.name: --layer-license-info
options.description: License info to set on the Lambda layer

options.name: --package-manifest
options.description: Package manifest for a "runtime-package-store" layer that indicates the NuGet packages to add to the layer.

options.name: -od
options.description: | --opt-directory                The directory under the /opt directory the contents of the layer will be placed. If not set a directory name /opt/dotnetcore/store/.

options.name: --enable-package-optimization
options.description: If true the packages will be pre-jitted to improve cold start performance. This must done on an Amazon Linux environment.

arguments.name: LAYER-NAME> THE NAME OF THE LAYER

options.name: --disable-interactive
options.description: When set to true missing required parameters will not be prompted for.

options.name: --region
options.description: The region to connect to AWS services, if not set region will be detected from the environment.

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to use to look up AWS credentials, if not set environment credentials will be used.

options.name: --profile-location
options.description: Optional override to the search location for Profiles, points at a shared credentials file.

options.name: --aws-access-key-id
options.description: The AWS access key id. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-secret-key
options.description: The AWS secret key. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-session-token
options.description: The AWS session token. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

commands.name: package
commands.description: Command to package a Lambda project either into a zip file or docker image if --package-type is set to "image". The output can later be deployed to Lambda with either deploy-function command or with another tool.

options.name: --disable-interactive
options.description: When set to true missing required parameters will not be prompted for.

options.name: --region
options.description: The region to connect to AWS services, if not set region will be detected from the environment.

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to use to look up AWS credentials, if not set environment credentials will be used.

options.name: --profile-location
options.description: Optional override to the search location for Profiles, points at a shared credentials file.

options.name: --aws-access-key-id
options.description: The AWS access key id. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-secret-key
options.description: The AWS secret key. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-session-token
options.description: The AWS session token. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: -pl
options.description: | --project-location             The location of the project, if not set the current directory will be assumed.

options.name: -cfg
options.description: | --config-file                  Configuration file storing default values for command line arguments.

options.name: -pcfg
options.description: | --persist-config-file          If true the arguments used for a successful deployment are persisted to a config file.

options.name: -c
options.description: | --configuration                Configuration to build with, for example Release or Debug.

options.name: -f
options.description: | --framework                    Target framework to compile, for example netcoreapp3.1.

options.name: --function-architecture
options.description: The architecture of the Lambda function. Valid values: x86_64 or arm64. Default is x86_64

options.name: --msbuild-parameters
options.description: Additional msbuild parameters passed to the 'dotnet publish' command. Add quotes around the value if the value contains spaces.

options.name: -fl
options.description: | --function-layers              Comma delimited list of Lambda layer version arns

options.name: -o
options.description: | --output-package               The zip file that will be created with compiled and packaged Lambda function.

options.name: -dvc
options.description: | --disable-version-check        Disable the .NET Core version check. Only for advanced usage.

options.name: -pt
options.description: | --package-type                 The deployment package type for Lambda function. Valid values: image, zip

options.name: -it
options.description: | --image-tag                    Docker image name and tag in the 'name:tag' format.

options.name: -df
options.description: | --dockerfile                   The docker file used to build the image. Default value is "Dockerfile".

options.name: -dbo
options.description: | --docker-build-options         Additional options passed to the "docker build" command.

options.name: -dbwd
options.description: | --docker-build-working-dir     The directory to execute the "docker build" command from.

options.name: --docker-host-build-output-dir
options.description: If set a "dotnet publish" command is executed on the host machine before executing "docker build". The output can be copied into image being built.

options.name: -ucfb
options.description: | --use-container-for-build      Use a local container to build the Lambda binary. A default image will be provided if none is supplied.

options.name: -cifb
options.description: | --container-image-for-build    The container image tag (with version) to be used for building the Lambda binary.

options.name: -cmd
options.description: | --code-mount-directory         Path to the directory to mount to the build container. Otherwise, look upward for a solution folder.

commands.name: push-image
commands.description:
Build Lambda Docker image and push the image to Amazon ECR.
To get help on individual commands execute:
dotnet lambda help <command>

options.description: When set to true missing required parameters will not be prompted for.

options.name: --region
options.description: The region to connect to AWS services, if not set region will be detected from the environment.

options.description: Profile to use to look up AWS credentials, if not set environment credentials will be used.

options.description: Optional override to the search location for Profiles, points at a shared credentials file.

options.description: The architecture of the Lambda function. Valid values: x86_64 or arm64. Default is x86_64

options.description: | --publish-options              Additional options passed to the "dotnet publish" command.

options.name: --docker-host-build-output-dir
options.description: If set a "dotnet publish" command is executed on the host machine before executing "docker build". The output can be copied into image being built.

options.description: | --local-docker-image           If set the docker build command is skipped and the indicated local image is pushed to ECR.

options.description: | --dockerfile                   The docker file used to build the image. Default value is "Dockerfile".

options.description: | --image-tag                    Name and optionally a tag in the 'name:tag' format.
